steps.title: Locate the antecedent
steps.content: The sentence right before the blank says, "Multitask learning is another approach." The blank starts the next sentence and refers back to this idea. "Multitask learning" is a singular noun phrase, so the pronoun must be singular.

steps.title: Apply pronoun-antecedent agreement
steps.content: A pronoun must match its antecedent in number. Singular antecedent $\Rightarrow$ singular pronoun. "It" is the only singular pronoun among the choices.

steps.title: Eliminate per choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — Those: plural demonstrative pronoun — disagrees in number with singular "Multitask learning." ✗
  • Choice B — They: plural personal pronoun — disagrees in number with the singular antecedent. ✗
  • Choice C — It: singular pronoun that agrees with "Multitask learning" and correctly stands in as the subject. ✓
  • Choice D — Some: plural/indefinite, implying multiple things — disagrees with the single approach just named. ✗
That matches Choice C — It.
